title: BUG: real-bg synth modes paste cohort heart WITHOUT excising the real image's heart (collision)
description: synth.py real-bg modes (bg_mode=partition, keep_real_bg/hybrid) composite a cohort synth heart onto a random real image that STILL CONTAINS its own real heart. hybrid: img=where(cohort_mask,synth,real) -> the real image's real heart sits OUTSIDE the cohort mask, survives intact, UNLABELED -> net trains on real data corrupted by an unlabeled 2nd heart (hybrid 0.77 is real-data-training sabotaged, NOT a synth result). partition: real heart smeared into a bright bg intensity tier. IMPACT: invalidates the partition(0.51)/hybrid(0.77) real-bg diagnostics + the 'procedural==real-bg' and 'structured bg +0.26' claims (VOID). Zero-real procedural/flat runs UNAFFECTED (zeros bg, no real img). FIX: to use a real image as bg, first EXCISE its heart via its own GT mask (cut heart region) + INPAINT the hole with surrounding tissue, THEN paste the cohort heart into the clean hole. Requires pairing each real bg image with its GT (available from store). Re-measure clean structured-real-bg after fix.
notes: CLEAN 2-SEED STRUCTURED-BG LADDER (2026-07-02, 1000-cohort, post-fix): procedural .561/.561 (mean .561, zero-real, TIGHT) | partition-excised .587/.591 (.589, real intensity shapes) | hybrid-excised .208/.238 (.223, COLLAPSED). FINDINGS: (1) full-synth ceiling ~.56 rock-solid. (2) hybrid COLLAPSE is diagnostic NOT 'structure hurts' - pasting cohort heart at frame-center onto a DIFFERENT patient's thorax = heart anatomically misplaced (in lung field) + intensity seam -> garbage spatial prior. XCAT wouldnt have this (heart in cavity by construction). (3) structured bg (partition) only +.028 over procedural = noise floor, marginal. RETRACTION: single-seed 'real bg lifts myo .45->.57' was NOISE; 2-seed myo ~equal (proc .45/.50 vs part .57/.51). CONCLUSION: 0.56 ceiling = anatomy/domain gap NOT bg structure. Whole-torso build (hpy) chases only ~.03 -> low priority. Real targets = myo fidelity + synth-real contrast gap (b6tb). Bug mirs FIXED (excise_heart, +.077 on partition), verified, ready to close.
close_reason: FIXED + verified. excise_heart removes the real image's own heart before it becomes bg for a pasted synth heart (strategy refactor: Background ABC + PartitionBg/HybridBg assume heart-excised real_img). Impact: partition real-bg .510(polluted)->.587(clean), +.077. Downstream verdict: structured bg barely beats procedural (+.03, noise); hybrid collapses on heart-misplacement (a separate placement issue, not this bug). Commit in synth-strategy-refactor.

title: Synth Tier 1: MRI-physics augmentation (bias field + Rician + histogram match)
description: Tier 1 AUGMENTATION (perturb real images) — the diversify force. Add MRI-physics transforms to cardioseg/training/augment.py, matching its GPU-batched per-sample + do_X prob-gating idiom: (1) smooth bias-field modulation (the N4 dual; can share the smooth-field idea from normalization/n4._smooth3d), (2) Rician noise (replaces/augments today's plain Gaussian — correct distribution for MR magnitude), (3) k-space ghost/spike via torch.fft. New AugCfg fields (bias strength+p, rician toggle, ghost p, spike p). NOTE: input is z-scored, so these are domain-randomization in z-space (not physics-exact) — fine for invariance. Histogram-MATCHING is NOT here: it's targeted harmonization (needs a vendor reference) -> belongs with qfz (normalization/Nyul). Measure cross-vendor Dice delta (held-out Canon/ACDC) vs no-physics-aug baseline (ACDC 0.884/Canon 0.84). M&Ms-validated. Docs: cardioseg/preprocessing/normalization/README.md (taxonomy).
design: Build each factor as a physics model usable BOTH directions (augment + normalize) — see research/diversity_factors.md registry. Bias field: build smooth-field generator for aug, N4 (its inverse) for strip, A/B strip-vs-diversify on this axis. Don't strip+augment the same factor. Rician/ghost/spike = diversify only.
acceptance_criteria: bias-field + Rician + histogram-match wired into training; cross-vendor Dice measured vs no-aug baseline; throughput impact noted
close_reason: CONCLUDED (negative, measured). Bias-field aug regressed unseen-vendor (Canon 0.839->0.827, ECE up, error-detect down = confidently-wrong). Headroom small: BALD ~31% reducible (TTA), 4-seed deep ensemble ~15-18% (seeds agree); ensembling buys ~nothing (Canon +0.000/GE +0.006). Cross-vendor gap is mostly aleatoric + model-class, not aug-reducible. Lever = stronger model class (nnU-Net/3D). Code kept (transforms off by default). Documented: cardioseg/preprocessing/normalization/README.md Findings. Strip-dual (N4) = active counter-experiment.

title: Port MRXCAT painter lessons to our bSSFP engine: high-res paint + k-space PSF + uniform myo
description: DAG: our anatomy -> OUR painter, improved (bare-bones path). MRXCAT achieves tight real-like myo (real sigma vendor-stable .43-.52) by (a) UNIFORM/flat myo tissue value (fixLVTexture meanLV) and (b) HIGH-RES 920px anatomy -> proper k-space PSF downsample, NOT a crude gaussian blur. Our myo OVER-SPREAD (~4x real, diagnosed bd b6tb + synth_fidelity variance_attribution) traces to LOW-RES voxelization + gaussian blur smearing the thin 4px wall into bright blood/dark bg. FIX synth.synthesize_from_labels: paint at higher internal res then k-space-PSF downsample (we have the kspace op); keep myo within-class texture minimal/uniform. VERIFY myo pooled/per-slice sigma -> real vendor band via synth_fidelity --mode variance (VAL, leak-free), THEN 1 confirming 2-seed training run. No external deps (on-thesis). Refs b6tb findings + hpy MRXCAT triage. The capable-generator-taught-us payoff, applied.
notes: PREMISE REFUTED BY ANALYSIS (2026-07-03, analysis-first, no training). The MRXCAT high-res+k-space-PSF lesson is the WRONG lever for our myo: (1) variance_attribution knob decomposition on myo per-slice std: inflow +.379 (but inflow HELPS Dice, keep), sweep +.035, and blur/texture/noise/pv/bias ALL ~0 or NEGATIVE -> the corruption chain PSF/blur targets contributes ~zero to myo spread. (2) DEEPER: stripping corruption makes myo z-std WORSE not better (flat_bg 1.94, all-off 3.37 vs default 1.17) -> because per-slice Z-SCORE divides by IMAGE std; clean/flat images have small image-std -> myo z-std amplified. So 'synth myo sigma 4x real' is LARGELY A Z-NORMALIZATION ARTIFACT (synth vs real differ in global composition -> different image-std -> per-class sigma NOT comparable). This is the z-norm confound the owner flagged earlier + I under-weighted. IMPLICATION: don't port PSF/high-res (wrong lever, confounded target); the only clean cross-image metric is d' (affine-invariant ratio) which PSF doesn't move either. No cheap analytically-supported myo fix -> consistent with generation-ceiling being robust (myo weak link not knob-fixable). ck0n as-premised = closed/refuted. If myo is ever revisited, target d' SEPARABILITY not sigma, and expect the ceiling is largely real. Analysis-first saved a wasted training run.
close_reason: Premise refuted by analysis-first (no training): MRXCAT PSF/high-res lesson is the wrong lever for our myo. Corruption chain (blur/pv/texture) contributes ~0 to myo spread; and 'myo sigma 4x real' is largely a z-normalization artifact (clean images -> small image-std -> amplified per-class z-sigma; strip-all gives 3.37 vs default 1.17). The only clean cross-image metric is d' (affine-invariant), which PSF doesn't move either. No cheap myo fix -> myo ceiling largely real. z-norm confound (owner-flagged) confirmed.

title: MRXCAT whole-FOV source: keep XCAT surrounding-tissue labels multi-class + paint them
description: DAG entry point = anatomy(whole-FOV) + tissue -> one path to the input node (composite thesis; many paths lead to input, GENERATION.md). MRXCAT .vti carries 30+ surrounding-tissue labels (lung/liver/chest wall/bone) that core/data/dynamic/mrxcat.to_canonical currently collapses to bg. Instead keep them as a MULTI-CLASS tissue map and paint each by its tissue (extend the painter's whole-FOV bg handling) so the synth image has realistic surrounding anatomy = MRXCAT's ACTUAL edge over the heart-only SSM pool. The seg TARGET stays canonical 4-class; the extra labels drive the painted CONTEXT only. Uses the BUNDLED example data (no Duke, no warper run). Alternative DAG path, does NOT replace our own painter. Builds on the mrxcat.py adapter (hpy). Priority: do BEFORE the SSM-through-MRXCAT combo.
notes: TIGHT-WINDOW RETEST COLLAPSED -> WHOLE-FOV TRAINING IS A DEAD-END AS BUILT (2026-07-03). heart-only ~.52, whole-FOV loose(scale3) ~.39, whole-FOV tight(scale1.5) ~.09 (myo .05 = degenerate, both seeds). Tighter framing made it WORSE not better -> NOT a framing confound; the whole-FOV bg=mrxcat path itself produces unstable/garbage training. Likely: muddy surrounding contrast (lung/liver/muscle bSSFP too close to heart) + 1 anatomy -> heart not separable from painted organs -> net degenerates. Possible subtle bug in the tight collapse (not fully understood) but the direction clearly isn't paying off; NOT worth more compute. CONCLUSION: whole-FOV as a TRAINING source = dead-end. KEEP the source code (mrxcat.to_tissue_map/FovBg/build_fov_pool - adapter valid, geometry verified) for potential non-training use (context viz / the digital twin / if paired with real anatomy diversity later), but do NOT compose it into training. q4ww training-value = NEGATIVE. Heart-only MRXCAT anatomy (~.52, 1 anatomy ~= 1000-heart SSM) is the useful takeaway: anatomy diversity saturates fast, shape count is not the bottleneck. Mark q4ww done (built+evaluated, negative).
close_reason: Whole-FOV source built + verified + trained; training value NEGATIVE (regresses/collapses vs procedural bg: .52 -> .39 loose -> .09 tight). Source code kept (valid, geometry-verified) for non-training use; not composed into training. Key takeaway: 1 MRXCAT anatomy ~= 1000-heart SSM (~.52 vs .56) -> anatomy diversity saturates, shape count not the bottleneck.

title: Replace unphysical jitter with per-sample physical T1/T2/PD sampling (literature mean±range)
description: SynthCfg.jitter (=0.4) is an unphysical post-hoc Gaussian on the computed bSSFP signal ('residual per-class perturbation, extra breadth') AND a magic constant. But it carries ESSENTIAL variance: jitter=0 collapses zero-real cross-vendor from .564 to ~.28 (2026-07-02, zj0_s0 RV.166/myo.236/cav.426). Its ROLE (inter-subject relaxation + contrast breadth beyond the TR/flip/field sweep) is real; its FORM is a hack. FIX (same pattern as inflow replacing blood_scale): sample T1/T2/PD per-sample per-class from literature mean ± physiological SD (Stanisz 2005 / Bojorquez 2017, already cited in mri_physics.TISSUE) so the signal variance emerges from PHYSICS. Parameterless-in-spirit (params are cited literature spreads, NOT fitted to our data -> no dataset-statistic/test leak). Then retire jitter (and legacy blood_scale, the fitted 1.6). ACCEPTANCE: physical param sampling reproduces >= jitter's zero-real Dice (~.56) with NO magic breadth constant; selection/validation on VAL (acdc domain-shift split) only, Canon+GE test touched ONCE for the final triad.
design: Add per-class (T1sd,T2sd,PDsd) to TISSUE table from the cited reviews; in tissue_params or synthesize_from_labels draw params ~ N(mean, sd) per sample before bssfp_signal. Physiological ranges bound the draw (clamp to literature min/max). Remove jitter term (line ~342) and blood_scale.
notes: DE-CONFOUNDED over-spread (2026-07-03, added real_spread_bands to synth_fidelity: per-vendor σ band, target=ALL labelled real not one cohort; --val-only opts back). Owner pushback correct: blanket '4x over-spread' was wrong. Real per-vendor pooled-σ BANDS vs synth baseline: bg .79-.96 vs .96 (WITHIN), LV-cav .96-1.32 vs 1.42 (just over), RV .84-1.16 vs 1.32 (modest), LV-myo .43-.52 vs 1.37 (2.6x widest vendor). So bg/RV/cav synth σ ~ within the multi-vendor band = DR COVERING the range, NOT a defect. ONLY myo is genuinely over-spread, and it's a CLEAN defensible target: (a) real myo σ is vendor-STABLE .43-.52 (every scanner makes myo a tight band), (b) myo-SPECIFIC (bg/cav within-band -> NOT a global z-norm artifact, else all classes inflate together). Mechanism = thin-wall partial-volume: 4px myo band = mostly boundary voxels mixing bright-blood/dark-bg -> intra-myo spread no real vendor shows. KEY: reducing myo over-spread toward the vendor band is UNPHYSICAL-artifact removal, NOT cutting real diversity (unlike the CV-cut which cut the real across-slice sweep) -> principled, doesn't trade OOD. Candidate fix = sharper myo-boundary paint / higher effective resolution / PV model that doesn't smear the thin wall. Confirm any fix with ONE val training run.

title: Synth augmentation in the DATA-SCARCE regime (single-vendor / few-patient real + synth)
description: pwih showed synth-aug NEUTRAL vs ABUNDANT multi-vendor real (.8505 vs .854). But augmentation earns its keep when real is SCARCE/single-domain - the regime where synth fills what real lacks. TEST: restrict real train to (a) single vendor (e.g. Siemens only) or (b) few patients (e.g. 20-50), train real-alone vs real+synth-mix (anatomy_mode=mix, force_synth already wired), eval cross-vendor 2-seed. WIN = synth-mix beats scarce-real-alone (esp on the UNSEEN vendors/pathology). This is the honest deployable value of the synth artifact - domain-randomization aug matters most when real coverage is thin. Reuse train.py --set generator.data.test_vendors/n_patients to make real scarce.

Augmentation is a dead end ON THIS BENCHMARK (mild cross-vendor contrast gap). Synth value stays: no-annotation/unseen domains + inverse twin, NOT augmenting real (rich or scarce) here.

title: FIT extend: tissue T1/T2/PD recovery with identifiability (multi-frame / priors)
description: Extend the FIT probe (bd ncph) from acquisition-only to per-tissue T1/T2/PD. Single bSSFP frame is under-determined (TR/flip/T1/T2 trade off) -> need identifiability: (a) fit across MULTIPLE frames/acquisitions of the same subject (real qMRI approach - varied flip/TR disentangle T1 vs T2), or (b) tissue PRIORS (literature T1/T2 +- range) regularizing the fit. OUTPUT: per-tissue param estimate = the qMRI/digital-twin product. Depends on the FIT probe landing first.
notes: PREMISE VALIDATED + SHARPENED (2026-07-02 synthetic loss-surface test): the lever is SHARED ABSOLUTE SCALE across the fit, NOT frame count. Per-frame normalization (what uncalibrated MRI forces on a lone scan) -> tissue T2 unidentifiable at ANY K (61/61 candidates fit equally, even K=3). Shared absolute scale (no per-frame norm) -> unique recovery (argmin=true, 1/61) even K=1. So multi-acquisition works ONLY because frames from ONE SESSION share receiver gain -> normalize the SET JOINTLY, never per-frame. IMPL CONSTRAINT for the FIT: fit >=2 frames that share scale (same session/gain), joint-normalize the set once. Even K=2 suffices. bالسSFP-only recovers T2/T1-ratio+PD (contrast), not T1,T2 separately - note for the qMRI claim. De-risks building the multiframe FIT API.

title: FIT probe: differentiable render(mask,θ) + fit ACQUISITION (TR/flip) to one real scan given its seg
description: First FIT operator (bd ncph, identifiable subset). Build a DETERMINISTIC differentiable render(mask, theta) reusing mri_physics.bssfp_signal + one-hot paint (NO stochastic corruption) so it's differentiable wrt theta. START identifiable subset: theta = {TR, flip} (2 scalars, tissue T1/T2/PD held at literature values), fit to ONE real scan given its segmentation by Adam minimizing MSE(render, real) on labeled voxels (+ optional per-class W1). OUTPUT: fitted TR/flip + recon-vs-real montage + recon error = the 'is it the same' test. Doubles as a forward-model VALIDITY probe: low recon = physics spans this scan; high residual = what physics is missing. Identifiability: single frame under-determines full qMRI (TR/flip/T1/T2 trade off) - so fit the SUBSET first, note it, dont claim full qMRI yet.
close_reason: PROBE COMPLETE (blocked-by-ncph is just the open umbrella). FIT operator built+tested; KEY FINDING: acquisition NOT identifiable from one-frame heart region (2 tissues, affine absorbs contrast) -> multi-acquisition REQUIRED (5ev5 promoted P2). inverse.py reusable.

title: Controlled/inverse generation: fit painter params to a real recording (differentiable digital twin)
description: The SECOND pipeline family (owner 2026-07-02). Forward/training synth = labels -> RANDOMIZED params -> image, objective DIVERSITY (have it). This is the INVERSE: (1) poll a real recording, (2) RECONSTRUCT it through parameters - fit the generator's physics params (acquisition TR/flip/field + per-tissue T1/T2/PD + bg) by backprop through the DIFFERENTIABLE painter (synthesize_from_labels is pure torch) to minimize |synth(params) - real_scan| given the scan's segmentation (labels say which voxel = which tissue). OUTPUT: interpretable qMRI params for the scan + a reconstruction. Objective here is FIDELITY (opposite of training's diversity) - W1/recon-error IS the goal. USES: qMRI parameter estimation, harmonization (re-render at canonical params), per-scan digital twin = the Corsmed-differentiated artifact. ARCH: reuse the forward painter unchanged; add an inverse/fit module (torch optim over a MatchedAcq-like param container + tissue table). Shared physics engine, two directions. START: fit acquisition-only (TR/flip/field) to one real scan given its seg, check recon; then extend to per-tissue T1/T2/PD.
design: One forward model, two objectives. Inverse = torch.optim over param tensors, loss = recon MSE (+ optional per-class W1) on labeled voxels. Differentiable painter already supports it. MatchedAcq is the forward param container; inverse fits those params.

title: Synth-vs-real EMBEDDING coverage analysis (does synth encompass real?)
description: Owner idea (2026-07-02, 'for later'): build an embedding space (e.g. encoder features / learned rep) and project BOTH synth-generated and real slices into it to SEE whether the synth distribution ENCOMPASSES the real one (coverage), not just per-class intensity W1 (synth_fidelity, which is shape-agnostic). Harder now that anatomy pool hearts dont match real masks - a spatial/holistic embedding is the right tool. Deliver: 2D projection (UMAP/PCA of embeddings) overlaying synth vs real + a coverage metric (real points inside synth support / density ratio). Answers 'is our synth generator covering the real manifold or leaving gaps'. Complements synth_fidelity (marginal per-class) with a JOINT/holistic view.
notes: APICAL SKEW FOUND + FIXED (2026-07-02): the 24% synth 'fan beyond real' = apical/basal slices ~all myo no cavity (fracMyo .96, fracLVcav 0, myo/cav 269, small). Mesh contributes whole apico-basal stack -> apical myo-only over-represents 11x vs real (19.4% vs 1.8%). Added build_pool min_cav_frac=0.05 (drop cavity-poor apical). Filter effect: synth_beyond_real 0.24->0.075 (over-spread cleaned), real coverage 0.78 HELD. Building pool_1000_bal.npz; retrain zero-real on it vs 0.56 pending GPU (after 5r7n). Note: does NOT fix the 22% real-tail undercoverage (pathology -> vpn5); only removes synth junk over-spread + skewed training targets.

title: Synth anatomy as augmentation: real+synth mix vs real-alone (deployable win)
description: The DEPLOYABLE payoff of synth anatomy (bwp). Zero-real hit ~0.56 (great research result, frontier unwalled) but structurally can't beat real-trained 0.86. The real value: mix the 1000 coverage-by-construction synth anatomies (Zenodo 4506930, pool_1000.npz, 12304 slices) INTO real training (ACDC+M&M) as a robustness multiplier. GOAL: does real+synth-aug lift CROSS-VENDOR held-out Dice above real-alone 0.86? This is the Corsmed/flagship story - synth anatomy as domain-randomization augmentation, not replacement. PLAN: (1) baseline real-alone 2-seed cross-vendor (default split: test cmrxmotion+Canon/GE). (2) real + synth-anatomy mix at ratios (e.g. synth_p or explicit pool concat 25/50/75%), painted by procedural OR real-bg partition. (3) 2-seed each, cross-vendor. Win = +delta > noise (.02-.03) over real-alone, esp on RV/myo OOD (the classes that collapse cross-vendor per cross-dataset-generalization memory). Needs train.py path to MIX anatomy_pool with real (currently anatomy_pool REPLACES real train). Noise discipline throughout.
design: Extend train.py: anatomy_pool as ADDITIVE (concat synth labels+real masks) not replacement, with a mix ratio. Or route via Generator synth_p with pool as extra mask source. 2-seed cross-vendor vs real-alone.
notes: VERDICT (2026-07-02, 2-seed): real+synth MIX .851/.850=.8505 vs real-alone .858/.850=.854 = NEUTRAL (-.003 noise, per-class identical RV.87/myo.80/cav.88). Synth-aug does NOT beat ABUNDANT multi-vendor real (real already generalizes at .854; ~0.56-quality synth anatomy adds no coverage the real set lacks). Wiring committed (anatomy_mode=mix, force_synth). The FAIR aug test = DATA-SCARCE regime (single-vendor / few patients) where synth fills domains real lacks -> filed follow-up. Synth value stays: no-annotation/unseen domains + inverse twin, NOT augmenting rich real.

title: Synth anatomy: myocardium fidelity — push zero-real ceiling
description: Zero-real synth anatomy (1000-cohort, procedural bg, physics) landed ~0.56 cross-vendor 2-seed (bwp). Per-class: LV-cav STRONG (.71-.75), RV mid (.48), MYOCARDIUM the WEAK LINK (.46-.50). Myo is thin-walled -> partial-volume averaging + bSSFP contrast fidelity dominate. GOAL: raise synth myo Dice toward LV-cav levels, pushing the zero-real ceiling from ~0.56 into low-0.6s. INVESTIGATE: (1) synth myo WALL THICKNESS vs real (voxelized Rodero myo may be too thin/thick after scale-norm; measure real ACDC myo thickness px distribution, compare to synth). (2) myo CONTRAST - myocardium bSSFP signal vs blood/bg under the swept params; is the myo-blood and myo-bg contrast separable enough? (3) partial-volume sigma tuning for the thin wall. (4) RV recovery quality (still .48). Measure real-vs-synth per-class W1 (synth_fidelity by class). Noise discipline: 2-seed, deltas <.02-.03 = noise. Diversity is SATURATED (20==1000), so this is contrast/geometry fidelity, NOT more meshes.
design: Zero-real purist track: keep anatomy_pool + procedural bg; vary myo geometry (thickness) + physics (contrast, pv_sigma). Compare 2-seed cross-vendor vs current ~0.56 baseline.
notes: INFLOW-ONLY CONFIRMED WIN (2026-07-02, 2-seed zero-real cross-vendor Canon+GE, pool_1000_bal+procedural). base .375/.456/.699 mean .510 -> inflow .470/.504/.717 mean .564 (+.054). Per-class both-seed-consistent: RV +.095 (robust, deltas +.070/+.121), myo +.048 (both +, .074/.021), LV-cav +.018 (noise). SECOND independent 2-seed verification of inflow (first: memory +.05-.075). ISOLATES: inflow is the real lever; the CV-cut sank the combined fix. WORKS BY DIVERSITY not fidelity: physiological through-plane velocity sweep -> varied blood brightness/flow = better cross-vendor generalization (RV=most-gained class, matches the rv|bg d' .83->1.86 lift). ACTION: SynthCfg.inflow default is False -> should be True for zero-real (2x verified). NB confirm it doesn't hurt synth+real DA before flipping the global default (DA is the deployable path). STOPPED tuning myo per diversity>fidelity: myo|blood separability is learnable (not a bug), gap-to-real is fidelity (not the objective). Close b6tb as: geometry ruled out, contrast-separability characterized, inflow banked, no further knob-tuning warranted.
close_reason: Diagnosed + banked. Myo weak link = contrast-separability (geometry ruled out: thickness matches real 3.82/4.21px; brightness levers d'-invariant). Separability learnable (not a bug); gap-to-real is fidelity, not the training objective (diversity>fidelity) -> not knob-fixable, don't chase. Real lever found: inflow (physical DR) +.054 zero-real 2x-verified, neutral DA -> flipped SynthCfg.inflow default True. Stopped tuning per principle. Deployable DA .851 unchanged. ~0.56 zero-real ceiling robust.

title: Synth anatomy T2: MRXCAT2.0 phantom (MRI-native, pathology sweeps + whole-FOV)
description: FULL build after Rodero probe validates generated anatomy. MRXCAT2.0 (ETH, biomed.ee.ethz.ch/mrxcat): cardiac-MRI phantom, VAE LV shape + explicit pathology (DCM/HCM/infarct via LV mass/EF/wall/strain/elliptical-scar) + XCAT torso whole-FOV. Use anatomy label maps (fast) not the ~2h full sim - our painter does contrast. title: Synth anatomy T1: Rodero SSM -> voxelize -> fit-analysis -> pure-synth train
description: FAST PROBE of generated anatomy (fail-fast before MRXCAT). Adopt Rodero/King's 4-chamber SSM (Zenodo CC-BY, +-2/3 SD mode-extrapolation = coverage-by-construction). Steps: (1) locate+download the exact Rodero SSM asset, verify format/license; (2) voxelize meshes -> canonical labels (RV=1/myo=2/LV-cav=3) + label-remap check (CT-derived 4-chamber -> our MRI 3-class; drop atria or map); (3) generate anatomy -> diffeomorphic deform (now fixed) -> physics painter; (4) ANALYSE FIT generated-vs-real (synth_fidelity per-class W1 + by_vendor; does generated anatomy match real shape/intensity distribution?); (5) pure-synth TRAIN + cross-vendor eval vs current pure-synth 0.68. Healthy-only OK (flagship per-frame, no temporal; we have real pathology in real masks). Caveats: CT chamber-def mismatch, PCA extrapolation implausible past ~3SD (validity gate). Order: this BEFORE mrxcat.
notes: FIRST RUN = mis-calibrated 0.07 (2026-07-01), architecture PROVEN, cohort choice WRONG. Pipeline works end-to-end: mesh->SAX voxelizer (verified textbook anatomy)->pool(485 slices)->train-on-synth/eval-real held-out (zero real data). But cross-vendor 0.071 = FAILED, diagnosed compound: (1) WRONG COHORT - used extreme 4593739 = 39 +-SD perturbations of ONE mean shape (tail-coverage, ~1 shape family, 485 near-dup slices) -> segmenter memorizes single anatomy, fails on varied real. The DIVERSE base is 4590294 (1000 healthy hearts) - grabbed wrong Zenodo. (2) bg_mode=flat forced (no real img) = known ~-0.27 regression. (3) scale: Rodero heart bbox 47px vs real 63px (~25% smaller in-frame). FIX PATH: use 4590294 1000-cohort subset (~200, ~1min/mesh) for base diversity; restore bg realism (generate, or accept partition needs a bg source); scale-match voxelization (larger inplane or heavier scale-aug). Then retrain. Voxelizer + pool + train-branch all committed + working. NOTE: Windows-path gotcha - Python needs D:/ not /d/ mount (tar/find use /d/).
close_reason: PROBE COMPLETE (fail-fast). Voxelizer built+verified (Rodero mesh->SAX 3-class, textbook anatomy). Pure-synth-anatomy measured: flat-bg 0.07, real-bg 0.39, vs real-mask+partition 0.66, real-trained 0.86. DOUBLE-GATED: bg wall (flat->real +0.32) AND synth anatomy inferior to real masks (-0.27 same-bg: correlated extreme cohort/scale/CT-MRI defs/RV-fill). Heavy build (1000-cohort + whole-FOV bg-gen) NOT justified by this - payoff far + multi-axis + uncertain ceiling. anatomy.py reusable if frontier revisited. hpy(mrxcat) shares the bg wall. Real levers stay model-class + learned generator (ap6, joint bg+anatomy).

title: Synthetic anatomy generation (version A: adopt MRXCAT2.0 / UK Biobank HeartSSM + deform + physics painter)
description: Generate diverse, anatomically-VALID label maps (RV/myo/LV-cav) -> feed existing physics painter -> train segmenter. Currently synth uses real GT masks + mild deform=0.15 (low shape variability); this is the anatomy frontier. SOTA (deep-dive 2026-07-01_cardiac-anatomy-generation-sota): 3 families - SSM/PCA (UK Biobank HeartSSM, Auckland atlas; linear, pathology-extrapolation breaks), PARAMETRIC PHANTOM (MRXCAT2.0 = cardiac-MRI SOTA: VAE LV shape + pathology knobs healthy/DCM/HCM/infarct via LV-mass/EF/strain/wall-thickness + XCAT torso for surroundings), LEARNED (VAE-GAN/diffusion; mode-collapse+topology violations). VERDICT: parametric+learned COMPLEMENTARY (coverage vs realism) - validates our parametric-sweep+deform+learned-plausibility plan; SOTA = parametric-first then hybrid. ADOPT don't build: MRXCAT2.0 (biomed.ee.ethz.ch/mrxcat), HeartSSM (github baiwenjia/ukbb_cardiac, UK-Digital-Heart-Project/Statistical-Shape-Model). PLAN: (T1) adopt MRXCAT2.0/HeartSSM -> base anatomy label maps -> parameterize pathology (ranges from our real_levels/RESULTS) -> diffeomorphic deform (SynthSeg-style) -> our physics painter; validate OOD on MMWHS/HVSMR-2.0. (T2) hybrid + whole-FOV (XCAT torso lung/liver) + TPM Loss topology. CAVEATS: pathology params manual; whole-FOV unsolved (torso generic); topology fragile (TPM Loss 2025, cardiac-unvalidated); MRXCAT full sim ~2h/img but anatomy-only=seconds (we only need labels). First: check deform-sweep result - does MORE shape variability even help before investing.

Delivered: distinct-cohort voxelizer (real 20 + SSM 1000, elemTag+geometric-SAX), measured-real scale-norm, robust RV recovery, procedural zero-real bg, parallel build_pool. RESULT: zero-real synth anatomy 0.07 -> ~0.56 cross-vendor (probe's walled verdict WRONG). Diversity saturates at 20 (1000 adds stability not ceiling). Commits c551f50/578b263/+parallel. FOLLOW-ONS (both part of final version, owner): b6tb=myo fidelity (push zero-real ceiling, myo weak .46-.50), pwih=synth-as-augmentation (real+synth vs real-alone 0.86, the deployable win). bwp stays open as umbrella until both land.

title: Blood-anchored normalization (two-point affine, composition-robust) — cross-vendor
description: First NON-NULL harmonization signal. Two-point affine normalization (air->0, blood->1) on a physical reference, vs z-score. Cheap gate (GT oracle): cross-vendor MYO level spread 0.558 (z-score) -> 0.262 (blood-norm), RV spread ~0. Beats z-score because z-score mean/std is composition-sensitive (FOV/bg fraction varies by vendor); blood/air anchors are physical + composition-robust -> remove the composition-driven cross-vendor shift. Survives the fat/reliability critique via: (a) MODE/reference not max (fat can be brighter, fine - blood=1, fat=1.5); (b) LINEAR scale, divide (gain is multiplicative); (c) two-point affine (air trivially findable, blood via heart-crop+mode). NEXT: oracle train A/B (GT-blood-norm vs z-score, cross-vendor Dice + myo-coupling) - decides if reduced spread converts to Dice (instance-norm may already capture it). IF yes: cascade (coarse blood/air seg -> normalize -> fine heart seg) for inference-time blood estimate; validate estimator vs GT esp. small cavities (ES/HCM = failure risk). Ref harmonization discussion 2026-07-01; white-stripe cardiac analog.
close_reason: ORACLE NULL (2026-07-01). Blood-anchored two-point affine (air->0/blood->1) halved cross-vendor tissue-level spread (myo 0.558->0.262) - real input effect, unlike Nyul. BUT oracle (GT blood, upper bound) cross-vendor Dice 0.861->0.862 = +0.001 NULL. Why: InstanceNorm already removes composition/scale variance per-instance -> input harmonization redundant. Tested the ceiling -> inference cascade NOT worth building. norm=blood kept as documented-null tool. Capstone: even physically-correct oracle-perfect harmonization is null because model self-normalizes. 4 independent confirms harmonization isn't the lever (inverse ill-posed, Nyul null, no clean ref/fat, oracle blood null). Real levers: model-class + learned generator (ap6).

title: Machine-conditioned generation: wire acquisition_for + inflow enhancement (replace global blood_scale)
description: blood_scale=1.6 is a global scalar fit to the AVERAGE fidelity gap. by_vendor measurement: blood-level (LV-cav location) gap is vendor-dependent, 2.3x spread (Siemens 0.77, GE 1.25, Philips 1.79), pure LOCATION, shape vendor-invariant+small -> physics contrast right, only blood LEVEL wrong by a machine-dependent amount. Audit: acquisition_for(vendor) is NEVER called in the paint - TR/flip sampled from global ranges, vendor is a cosmetic tag. So generation is not machine-conditioned. PRINCIPLED FIX (3 steps): (1) wire acquisition_for into paint - vendor -> its TR/flip (+jitter), not global range; (2) add inflow enhancement - bSSFP eq is steady-state, cine blood is partly fresh unsaturated inflow -> brighter; model TR-conditioned fresh fraction swept from physiological range (per-vendor by construction, NOT fit to test); (3) validate on external/untuned vendor (Canon or untuned M&Ms split). Deep-dive 2026-06-30 has the data + plan.
notes: PARTIALLY DONE (2026-07-02): inflow enhancement IMPLEMENTED (synth.py cfg.inflow, physiological v/thk/TR fresh-fraction, no magic) + acquisition RANDOMIZED strategy (RandomizedAcq: physics-derived TR/flip FWHM band) + MatchedAcq (per-machine). blood_scale SUPERSEDED by inflow. REMAINING: per-VENDOR acquisition_for wiring (vendor->its TR/flip, currently vendor is still a cosmetic tag under randomized/legacy; MatchedAcq matches ONE machine not per-sample-per-vendor) + validate on untuned vendor. Note: match-vs-randomize test showed randomize>match even in-domain (diversity>fidelity), so per-vendor MATCHING for TRAINING is likely not the win; per-vendor conditioning matters more for the INVERSE/twin (ncph). Re-scope accordingly.

title: Pure-synth: calibrate physics contrast to per-vendor acquisition (reference data)
description: The pure-synth systematic error is NOT invalid units - the bSSFP signal units are valid. It's that we use GENERIC acquisition params, so the contrast/composition doesn't match any real vendor -> cav lands at wrong z (fidelity investigation, deep_dives/2026-06-30). FIX (user insight): calibrate physics to per-vendor/field acquisition. Collect typical cine bSSFP params (TR/TE/flip, field) per vendor (Siemens/Philips/GE/Canon) as REFERENCE DATA (reference/acquisition.yaml, provenance; we already parse vendor+field per the normalization taxonomy) -> a normalization axis. Synth samples acquisition params from the vendor distribution (not generic ranges) -> contrast calibrated to real vendors -> composition matches -> should close the pure-synth cav gap while staying all-physics (one scale, no stats hybrid = avoids the scale-incompatibility of hd9). Measure: cav location down? pure-synth transfer up from 0.62? Ties to core.reference + normalization/ vendor parsing.
notes: CLOSED-refuted by the sim2real fit (core/data/analysis/sim2real.py): per-vendor heart contrast already matches across vendors (residuals ~0, best-fit params degenerate) -> per-vendor acquisition calibration is NOT the fidelity lever. The gap is composition/z-norm, not acquisition. See deep_dives/2026-06-30.
close_reason: Closed

title: Composition-matched bg: physics heart + data-matched background (close cav z gap)
description: The fidelity investigation (deep_dives/2026-06-30) proved the cav-too-bright gap is composition/z-norm: synth BG intensities (approximate physics) don't match real bg magnitudes, so per-image z-score lands the cavity at the wrong relative z. FIX: paint HEART by bSSFP physics (labeled, we have the model) but BG tiers to MATCH the real per-tier intensity mean/std (unlabeled, no tissue params). Closes cav location while keeping heart physical. Re-add per-tier real bg stats (removed in the collapse) for bg only; override bg-tier mu/sg. Measure: cav location down (was 0.9)? mean fidelity? attribution RV recall up? pure-synth transfer Dice vs 0.62/0.66? Goal: viable training on synth.
notes: TRIED physics-heart + data-matched-bg: FAILED (cav loc 0.9->1.76, mean 0.52->0.99). Root cause = SCALE INCOMPATIBILITY: bSSFP gives contrast in arbitrary signal units (~0.001-0.01); real-bg stats are z-units (~-1..1). Can't concatenate. Reconciling scales = data-anchoring the heart = the stats approach (couples heart<->bg). Finding: physics generation is RELATIVE/unscaled contrast; anchoring to real composition needs a data scale ref -> clean options are all-physics (0.62, composition gap) or all-stats (0.66, composed), NOT a hybrid. Reverted. => pure-synth caps ~0.62-0.66 by any paint; closing to real (0.86) needs a GAN (ap6). Reframe: productive 'keep training on synth' = synth-as-AUGMENTATION (mix w/ real), untested for a GAIN over baseline.

title: Physics: off-resonance / bSSFP banding (add Δf term to signal)
description: We use ON-resonance Freeman-Hill only. Real bSSFP signal depends on off-resonance Δf -> characteristic BANDING + signal spread from B0 inhomogeneity; blood (long T2) bands hardest -> our blood is unrealistically uniform. Add a smooth Δf (B0) field per sample + the full off-resonance bSSFP signal -> realistic blood/tissue variation+banding. The genuine bSSFP physics omitted; likely the cav-fidelity culprit. Measure: cav W1 down? attribution RV recall up? transfer Dice?
notes: DONE: banding() correct bSSFP physics (blood bands deeper than myo, tested), wired as cfg.b0_hz smooth Δf field. VERDICT: does NOT fix cav — worsens it (loc 0.89->1.48 @ b0=100). The cav location gap is in per-image z-score space; lowering blood signal gets re-referenced by normalization. => cav-too-bright is a COMPOSITION/normalization effect, NOT blood-signal. No blood-signal knob (flow, banding) closes it. Kept off-by-default as valid physics.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fidelity: location-vs-shape decomposition (is cav gap a normalization/bg artifact?)
description: cav W1 ~0.9 measured in per-image z-space. Decompose W1 into LOCATION (mean diff) vs SHAPE (centered W1). If location dominates, the gap is a z-shift from bg composition mismatch (fix bg, blood physics fine); if shape dominates, blood signal model is genuinely wrong. Cheap redirect before chasing blood physics. Add centered-W1 to synth_fidelity.py.
notes: RESULT: blood gap is LOCATION not shape. cav W1 0.93 = location 0.928 + shape 0.087 (shape ~perfect). RV similar (loc 0.58, shape 0.19). Blood SHAPE is right, just too BRIGHT by ~0.9z. NOT an architecture ceiling (earlier read was wrong) - physics-closable. myo/bg are the opposite (location fine, mild shape mismatch). -> off-resonance banding (bdr) lowers+spreads blood = the fix; unifies with the attribution shortcut (blood uniquely bright).
close_reason: Closed

title: Synth fidelity eval — distributional synth-vs-real distance (where/why it breaks)
description: Quantify how well the generator mimics real, per class, so we SEE where it breaks (and whether the physics architecture even CAN mimic real with small error, or if a GAN is needed). evaluation/synth_fidelity.py: generate synth from REAL masks, compare per-class intensity distributions real-vs-synth (Wasserstein-1 / quantile distance) -> which class's contrast is most off. Follow-up: a real-vs-synth DISCRIMINATOR AUC (0.5=indistinguishable, 1=easily separated = far) + attribute it to see WHAT gives synth away. Guides the harder-synth work (4w-around-heart): harden -> re-measure fidelity + RV recall. Closed loop with attribution.
notes: DONE: synth_fidelity.py (per-class Wasserstein synth-vs-real) built + tested. Verdict: blood worst-matched (cav W1 ~0.9, structural location/shape gap), myo/bg closer. Flow knob doesn't close it (within ±0.04 noise). => physics-paint has a fidelity CEILING on blood. Follow-ups (nice-to-have): average N draws for a stable metric; real-vs-synth discriminator AUC + attribute it. Closed loop with attribution.py.
close_reason: Closed

title: Harder synth around the heart — kill the bright-blob shortcut
description: Attribution (evaluation/attribution.py) on the pure-synth model shows it shortcuts on the bright central LV-cavity blob -> under-segments thin/off-center/low-contrast RV (recall 0.45->bg) + boundaries. Harden the peri-cardiac region so brightness isn't a valid shortcut: (1) bright CONFUSERS next to the heart - fat, aorta/vessels (unlabeled bright blood pools), pericardium - so 'bright != heart'; (2) lower RV contrast margin + partial-volume at the RV-lung-wall interface (realistically hard thin RV); (3) richer immediate peri-cardiac tissue (fat ring, vessels) vs the current far-bg partition. Closed loop: synth->train->attribution->harden->recheck confusion (RV recall up? saliency off the central blob?). bSSFP/physics; measure vs current pure-synth + watch RV recall.
notes: Tried the composition fix (vessel-bright bg ladder): DUD — cav location 0.89->0.88 (noise), mean fidelity slightly worse; reverted. Root cause is deeper (see research/deep_dives/2026-06-30_synth-fidelity-investigation.md): cav-too-bright is composition/z-norm — synth BG intensities (approximate tissue physics) don't match real bg magnitudes (only shapes via partition), so per-image z-score lands the cavity at wrong relative z. Closing it needs composition-MATCHED bg (data-driven bg paint, reintroduces stats) or a learned generator (ap6). Pure-synth caps ~0.62-0.66 with physics. Diagnostic loop + localized root cause = the deliverable.
close_reason: Closed

title: Adopt pydantic v2 at config + data boundaries (validated configs/manifests)
description: Roll out pydantic v2 where data crosses a trust boundary or needs runtime validation — turning silent-coercion dataclasses into validated models with clear errors. Today hparams.py dataclasses coerce --set overrides but DON'T bound-check (aug.gamma_p=5 trains 6 min on garbage); meta.csv / adapter meta() outputs aren't validated on read; paths.yaml isn't validated. Add pydantic dep (v2; pydantic-settings optional for env/file/CLI config).
design: Candidate sites, highest payoff first: (1) hparams TrainCfg/ModelCfg/AugCfg/DataCfg -> BaseModel: bound gamma_p/blur_p/val_frac in [0,1], epochs/batch>0, test/train_dataset as Literal/Enum, channels/strides validated tuples -> bad --set or config.json rejected at load with field-level error. (2) store meta.csv schema -> a SubjectMeta model validated on read (catch bad vendor/pathology/demographic values from sidecars). (3) adapter meta() return -> typed model (vendor enum, demographics ranges). (4) config.py paths.yaml -> validate data root. (5) any cardioview server/export manifest boundary. KEEP plain dataclasses/TypedDicts for purely-internal structs we fully control (validation = dead weight there); pydantic earns its keep only at edges + user/external-supplied input. v2 API: BaseModel, Field(ge=,le=), @field_validator, model_dump/model_validate.
acceptance_criteria: pydantic dep added; TrainCfg is a validated model (out-of-range --set/config rejected with clear message, not silently trained); meta.csv validated on store read; tests cover one accept + one reject case per validated model (equivalence-class style); existing runs still load (from_json back-compat).
close_reason: Pydantic v2 transition done where it pays: hparams.py (ModelCfg/AugCfg/LossCfg/DataCfg/TrainCfg) -> BaseModel with Field bounds + Literal enums + validate_assignment. Bad --set/config rejected at load with clear field error (verified: aug.gamma_p=5 -> ValidationError, no training). to_json/from_json via model_dump_json/model_validate_json (roundtrip-exact). pydantic>=2 added to deps. 8 accept+reject tests (41 total pass). Deliberately scoped to hparams (the trust boundary); store-meta + paths skipped (polars already typed, single path = marginal).

title: Check papillary/basal annotation convention ACDC vs M&M-2 (EF-bias source)
description: Suspected contributor to the -5.6% cross-dataset EF bias: ACDC and M&M-2 may DIFFER on whether papillary muscles count as LV blood-pool vs myocardium, and on basal-slice inclusion. That's a LABEL-protocol difference -> systematic EF offset that NO image normalization can fix. Probe: (a) geometric — measure papillary-muscle voxels inside LV-cav contour per dataset (are they labeled cav or myo?); (b) cross-ref the dataset papers (depends on the research task). If protocols differ, the honest fix is DOCUMENTING the convention mismatch, not bolting on a correction. Feeds the bias-calibration decision (cardiac-seg-lnd) and the model card.
notes: LITERATURE-ANSWERED (see 2026-06-20 deep-dive): ACDC/M&Ms share papillary convention (both include in LV cavity; M&Ms-2 follows ACDC standards). So this is NO LONGER a suspected bias source — downgrade to a cheap CONFIRMATION probe (geometric: are papillary voxels labeled cav in both?). Bias redirected to domain/intensity (qfz/lnd).
close_reason: Convention hypothesis REJECTED. EDV cross-domain pred/gt=1.01 -> ED cavity convention matches ACDC<->M&M-2 (papillaries included in both, per 2026-06-20 deep-dive). EF bias = systematic ESV over-prediction ~19% (over-fills small ES cavity: partial-volume + papillaries). Fixed-absolute mL -> inverse cavity-size scaling (corr -0.50; DCM unbiased -0.6, HCM -10.9). title: EF bias: report honestly + optional domain-adaptation demo (NOT a zero-shot subtraction fix)
description: Flagship has systematic EF bias -5.8% (LoA [-19,+7], ACDC-150). DO NOT 'fix' by subtracting a bias in the zero-shot/DG headline: (1) subtracting test-measured bias = test-label leakage; (2) even a leakage-free calibrator fit on a held-out split fails in DG — bias is domain-specific, so a source-domain (M&M-2 val) fit doesn't capture the target (ACDC) bias; capturing it needs target labels = converts zero-shot DG into domain ADAPTATION + shrinks the test set. So: (a) REPORT bias+LoA as a finding (-> model card d23); (b) the cross-centre volume-calibration gap IS a result, not a bug; (c) ONLY as an explicitly-fenced domain-adaptation side-experiment: 'with k labelled target cases, linear EF correction recovers X' — never folded into the zero-shot number. Real leakage-free fix lives in measure.py IF 4yf finds a convention/volume bug.
notes:
PROTOCOL (avoid test-set leak — the core danger): three DISJOINT sets. train=M&M-2 (seg), CALIBRATION=held-out M&M-2 split OR M&Ms-1 (fit a,b), test=ACDC (apply FROZEN a,b, report). Fitting a,b on ACDC and reporting on ACDC = leakage, number is meaningless. The real claim = correction fit on M&M-2-cal still helps on ACDC across the domain shift.

STEP 1 FIRST (cheap, eval-only, may kill the naive idea): bias-STRUCTURE diagnostic before fitting anything. (a) proportional bias — regress Bland-Altman diff vs mean-EF; if sloped, a constant -5.6% over-corrects low-EF (the sick patients) — dangerous. (b) subgroup bias — tabulate bias by VENDOR (have labels), pathology, field strength. A global a,b is only honest if subgroups agree; else correct per-vendor w/ CIs or don't deploy a global correction.

STEP 2: if bias ~constant + subgroup-stable -> fit a,b on held-out split, freeze, report raw+calibrated.

DOC: belongs in a Model Card (Mitchell et al. / FDA SaMD style), NOT just a README line — intended-use envelope (valid vendor/field range), quantitative raw-vs-calibrated stratified by vendor/pathology + CIs, caveats (out-of-envelope not corrected). Calibration is part of the device operating envelope. NOTE: spread (LoA +-15) is RANDOM error — calibration only moves the center/bias, won't touch spread (that's harmonization/better-masks).
close_reason: Bias reported honestly (cards) + step-1 diagnostic done: bias is proportional (slope -0.18/EF%, r-0.56, p<1e-13) + pathology-structured (DCM ~0 -> HCM -11.5%), so a global constant correction is dishonest (over-corrects DCM, under-corrects HCM). Report-only confirmed; any calibration must be per-subgroup = domain adaptation (needs target labels). Optional fenced DA demo left as a possible future experiment, not in the zero-shot number.

title: Error-distribution plots (KDE) for the model: boundary distances + EF Bland-Altman
description:
Motivation: every scalar metric (Dice, HD95, EF MAE) is a summary statistic that hides the error *distribution* — systematic offset vs sporadic outliers look identical in one number. Plot the distribution itself (the 'look at the KDE, not the mean' insight from the geometry/eval learning).

BUILD (suggest cardioseg/analysis/, the results-viz home):
1. Run the trained model on the 20 held-out val patients (reuse evaluation/validate predict_volume).
2. Boundary-distance distribution: per class (RV/myo/LV-cav), pool the per-surface-point distances pred<->GT (symmetric, via distance_transform_edt, in mm). KDE/histogram per class. A tight bump near 0 = good; a fat tail = outliers; a bump shifted off 0 = systematic offset.
3. EF agreement: per-patient (pred EF - GT EF). Bland-Altman plot (bias + 1.96sd limits of agreement) + a KDE of the residuals. THIS is the honest replacement for the bare 'EF MAE 3.1%' line.
4. Save figures to a gitignored outputs dir; reference one in the README Results (replace/augment the single-number claims).

WHY IT MATTERS: directly fixes two flagged gaps — (a) README conflates MAE with limits-of-agreement (E3); (b) we report one number, no distribution. Also surfaces whether errors are systematic (fixable) or outlier-driven (robustness).

ACCEPTANCE: a script that regenerates the figures from runs/acdc model; boundary-distance KDE per class + EF Bland-Altman saved; README Results cites the Bland-Altman (bias + LoA) instead of just MAE.

NICE-TO-HAVE: split distributions by pathology group (DCM/HCM/...) — HCM should show the fat tail.

title: Physics: through-plane partial volume + inflow enhancement (blood)

notes: DEPRIORITIZE: through-plane PV + inflow are blood-SIGNAL levers. Decomposition proved the cav gap is LOCATION (composition/z-norm), not signal-shape; flow+banding (both signal levers) failed to close it. Same logic => these likely won't either. The indicated lever is COMPOSITION (7co: bright peri-cardiac tissue raises image bright content -> blood less extreme in z + breaks the attribution shortcut). Revisit only if 7co implicates signal.

title: Generator physics: partial-volume boundaries + k-space PSF
description: Cheap physical stage-upgrades to the synth pipeline (each a one-stage swap, builds on partition). Baseline = pure-synth partition K6/jitter0.4 = 0.66 cross-vendor mean Dice. (1) PARTIAL VOLUME: real boundary voxels are tissue mixes; blur the class-MEAN map by ~0.5-1 vox before adding texture -> soft PV edges instead of hard label edges. (2) K-SPACE PSF: replace crude Gaussian blur with k-space truncation (fft -> low-pass window -> ifft) = real sinc PSF + slight Gibbs ringing = physical resolution. Measure each isolated vs 0.66, keep what helps. Order: PV then PSF.
notes: RESULT: no net gain. pv_sigma0.7=0.564 (hurts, over-blurs heart edges), kspace0.7=0.667 (neutral +0.007), pv+ks=0.661 (wash) vs 0.660 baseline. Cheap physical stage-upgrades plateau like predicted. Code kept (off by default) for completeness. Forward lever = bSSFP contrast (276), not these.
close_reason: Closed

Slots behind the existing Generator/GeneratorCfg seam. Risk: ACDC-vendor bias, mode collapse, may not beat the 0.774 hybrid ceiling. See README synth section + bgc.
notes: PARKED P3 (important, hard). The pure-synth gap to real (~0.66 vs 0.86) is composition/z-norm: physics reproduces contrast (sim2real residuals ~0) but synth bg magnitudes don't match real -> cavity at wrong relative z. All physics knobs ruled out (flow/banding/calibration/hybrid). Remaining levers: learned generator (GAN/diffusion label->image) OR composition-matched bg (data-driven bg paint, accepts stats-for-bg + a scale reconciliation). Owner: keep alive, close eventually. title: Soft loss: SoftDiceCE (soft Dice + soft cross-entropy)
description: MONAI DiceCELoss(to_onehot_y=True) argmaxes the CE target -> can't use for soft labels. Build SoftDiceCE(logits, soft_target[B,C,H,W]): soft Dice via MONAI DiceLoss(softmax=True, to_onehot_y=False) (accepts soft target) + soft CE = -(target*log_softmax(logits,1)).sum(1).mean(). fp32 under autocast. include_background handling. Keep soft as far down the pipeline as possible, collapse to hard only at the leaf: EF/volume from EXPECTED volume (collapse-never), mask/Dice thresholded at metric time. Honest expectation: calibration (ECE) = provable win (model is overconfident T=2.49); Dice ~neutral; EF bias plausibly better but GT-bound/unverifiable (GT is also hard, drawn on 10mm slices). Plumbing finding: MONAI DiceCELoss argmaxes the target for its CE term -> destroys soft labels -> need a custom soft loss.
close_reason: Soft run (σ=1.0) trained + measured. ECE 0.0925->0.0806 (-13%, calibration win) at equal Dice (0.902->0.900) + equal EF (MAE 6.50->6.47). title: Generator physics: bSSFP signal-equation contrast + T1/T2 reference
description: The high-leverage physical lever: replace statistical paint with the MRI signal equation. label -> literature tissue params (T1/T2/PD, 1.5T/3T) -> steady-state bSSFP signal = closed form of (T1,T2,TR,flip). Sweep TR/TE/flip/field per sample = PHYSICAL cross-vendor contrast diversity (correlated across tissues, the real vendor axis) instead of random jitter. T1/T2/PD = literature values in the reference store w/ citations (knowable->parsed). Closed-form steady-state, NOT full Bloch integration (KomaMRI overkill for cine). Builds on partition (shapes) + corruption chain. Measure vs 0.66 + vs the 4lu cheap upgrades. Was 'CMRsim full Bloch' (deprioritized); this is the light version that deep-dive didn't consider.
acceptance_criteria: label->tissue-param->CMRsim pipeline producing plausible synth cine; synth-physics training run; cross-vendor result vs Tier 1/2
notes: PRINCIPLED PATH found (2026-07-01). by_vendor shows blood-level gap is VENDOR-dependent (2.3x spread: Siemens 0.77/GE 1.25/Philips 1.79, pure location, shape vendor-invariant). Audit: acquisition_for NEVER wired into paint - vendor is cosmetic, TR/flip global. So blood_scale=1.6 fits the average; real fix = machine-conditioned generation + inflow enhancement (steady-state eq underestimates fresh inflow blood). Filed as its own bead. blood_scale kept as the empirical baseline that localized the mechanism.

title: [INVESTIGATION/parked] EF-objective loss refinement (volume-consistency)
description: EF bias -5.8% is entirely ESV over-prediction ~19% (4yf): model over-fills the small end-systolic cavity (partial-volume + papillaries mislabeled blood pool); fixed-absolute mL so it dominates small cavities (HCM ESV ratio 1.51, EF -10.9) and vanishes on large (DCM unbiased). EDV is accurate. This is the honest, leakage-free, domain-agnostic way to cut EF bias (vs the rejected EF-subtraction in lnd).
design: JOURNEY (2026-06-22): diagnosed EF bias = ESV over-seg (4yf). Loss-engineering options tried/considered: (1) HD-DT: CPU-only on Windows (cucim Linux-only) -> dead. (2) HER (GPU erosion-Hausdorff, built, committed): A/B NULL -- HD targets FAR boundary errors, ours is thin near-boundary over-fill + boundary already clean (largest-CC). (3) Tversky beta>alpha (built, dice_ce_tversky): mechanistically matches (penalize FP) BUT asymmetric weight = arbitrary knob + risks tuning-to-eval (test-leak concern). CONCLUSION = principled direction is OBJECTIVE ALIGNMENT not symptom-patch: add a volume-consistency term (soft per-class volume relative error) to Dice+CE. Directly optimizes the EF quantity, symmetric/self-balancing (relative error, no beta), leak-clean (encodes 'we care about volume', a priori). NEXT: build DiceVolLoss = DiceCELoss(raw logits) + lambda*|Vpred-Vgt|/Vgt on softmax(logits), include_background=False, fp32-under-autocast (volume sum overflows fp16), empty-class guarded. Per-slice volume = proxy for true 3D-chamber/EF (2D dataset; true EF needs ED/ES-paired batches).
acceptance_criteria: ESV pred/gt ratio toward 1.0 + EF bias reduced on ACDC-150, with LV-cav Dice + EDV ratio not regressed.
notes: REJECTED the loss-compensation approach (volume-consistency term) on principle (dimi 2026-06-29): the loss must stay a correct/balanced general objective, not be fit to a specific need. EF/ESV over-fill = compensate via BALANCED DATA, not a loss term. Volume-penalty = still symptom-fitting (arbitrary Dice-vs-vol weight, test-leak smell). Superseded by a data-balance approach: check if small-ES-cavity slices are under-represented in train; if so, balance (weighted sampling / scale-down aug) with loss untouched. Residual partial-volume floor = resolution/model-class lever, not loss.

Revisit if a bias-heavy dataset is ever acquired.

Fix RV cavity over-recovery in voxelize (RV/LV ratio 0.92 real vs 1.32 synth)
description: Static geometry panel (static_compare.py, bd uy4d) flags synth RV/LV cavity ratio 1.32 vs real 0.92 (W1 36) = synth RV cavity ~40% too large. Cause: voxelize RV recovery (binary_closing iters=3 on LV+RV wall union + hole-fill + keep-CC-touching-RV) inflates the RV cavity. The closing bridges gaps generously -> over-fills. FIX: tune closing iterations / use a tighter RV boundary, re-measure rv_lv_ratio toward 0.92. Intensity-W1 + Dice never showed this - only the geometry panel did. Verify with static_compare after.
notes: CLOSING REFUTED as cause (2026-07-02, direct sweep 25 meshes): rv_close 3/1/0 -> rv_lv 1.32/1.31/1.31 = closing iterations DON'T affect it. Real cause: RV cavity area over-recovered 1.48x vs real (synth 849 vs real 575 px) while LV-cav only 1.17x (706 vs 605). So the RV/LV ratio 1.32-vs-0.92 is disproportionate RV over-size, NOT a closing artifact. Likely (a) hole-fill captures the full LV+RV-enclosed lumen incl outflow, and/or (b) REAL modality/phase diff: Rodero=CT end-DIASTOLE (RV dilated) vs ACDC MRI. May be partly genuine anatomy, not a bug. DEPRIORITIZED P4 - not a quick fix, unclear if worth it (RV Dice already the weak class for other reasons). Revisit only if RV generation targeted.

title: Europe vs Asia population analysis (GT anatomy shift)

Compare GT-derived distributions: LV/RV cavity volumes (EDV/ESV), EF, LV-myo mass proxy, body size where available. Question: do Asian vs European hearts differ enough to be a modellable nuisance axis (something to normalize or augment for)? Honest caveat: confounded — Asia=single centre, healthy volunteers, Siemens 3T MAGNETOM Vida only; Europe=mixed pathology/vendor/centre. So any difference is population+acquisition tangled, NOT cleanly attributable. Descriptive only; report as observation, not proven population effect.
notes: LOWERED to P4 — data can't support the claim. Asia = cmrxmotion ONLY (n=69, all pathology 'other'/healthy volunteers, single vendor Siemens 3T Vida, single centre Shanghai, young 19-48). So 'continent' is COLLINEAR with 'which dataset': raw Euro-vs-Asia = healthy-young-Siemens vs all-comers (measures pathology mix + vendor, not geography). Even the fair healthy-only cut (Asia 69 vs Europe-normal 161) stays confounded by age+vendor+field+centre — can't isolate the Asian factor. Needs a genuinely multi-ethnic SINGLE-PROTOCOL cohort (same scanner/sequence across ethnicities) to ever be real; we have none. design: Candidate dedup strategies to explore (interesting territory): (1) metadata fingerprint = exact/near match on vendor+pathology+age+sex+height+weight; (2) image-content similarity = SSIM / perceptual-hash / downsampled-volume correlation on the ED frame; (3) registration-based mutual-information match score; (4) pretrained-encoder embedding distance; (5) ensemble the signals into a match-confidence, threshold, manual-verify the top candidates. NOISE vs SIGNAL discipline (owner called out, recurring mistake): single-seed cross-vendor Dice on this task scatters ~+-0.01 (measured: instance/none x zscore/blood ALL landed 0.851-0.870 = same number). Do NOT treat a single-seed delta < ~0.02-0.03 as a finding - it's noise. Require multi-seed replication before claiming any effect. Verified-real this session: pure-synth blood_scale/inflow +0.05-0.075 across 2 seeds (real). NOT real: harmonization +0.001, norm=none vs instance +0.009 (both noise). When many configs cluster within ~0.01, the honest conclusion is 'they're the same / this knob is flat', not a ranking. Stop reading signal into scatter.

SYNTH-VS-REAL HONEST GAP (measured 2026-07-02, IDENTICAL cross-vendor split cmrxmotion+Canon/GE, --quick 2-seed): real-trained TEST .858/.850=.854 (VAL .893), zero-real full-synth (1000-cohort procedural) TEST ~.56. Gap ~0.29 is REAL, NOT inflated: the 'real baseline overfits, fair OOD ~.70' hypothesis was REFUTED - real train set is multi-vendor (ACDC+M&M = 4 vendors) so this test isnt very OOD (matches cross-dataset-generalization: multi-vendor->generalizes). So zero-real full-synth WONT replace real training. Its value: (a) no-annotation/unseen domains, (b) AUGMENTATION real+synth vs real .854 (pwih, the deployable Q), (c) inverse digital-twin (ncph). myo is weakest class EVEN for real (.80 vs RV .88/cav .89) -> myo difficulty partly intrinsic, not just synth defect. Domain-rand fidelity-W1 is NOT an objective (diversity>fidelity); only location-shift is a fair bug-check.

MONAI boundary losses (HausdorffDTLoss) run on CPU on Windows -> ~0.3 batch/s (100x slower) + native segfault at scale. Cause: MONAI distance_transform_edt GPU path needs BOTH cupy AND cucim (transforms/utils.py use_cp = has_cp and has_cucim); cucim is Linux-only, no Windows wheel (PyPI cucim-cu13 = 3.9kB stub that fails to build). cupy alone insufficient. Workaround: use Tversky (FP-penalized, beta>alpha) for over-seg control — pure GPU region loss, same speed as Dice. HD-DT only viable on Linux.

METHODOLOGY (owner 2026-07-02, I violated it live): do NOT select configs/hyperparameters/features by reading HELD-OUT TEST Dice. I ran 6+ synth configs and picked inflow / judged jitter off Canon+GE test numbers = hyperparameter selection on the test set = leak; it invalidates the test as an honest generalization estimate. RULES: (1) touch the test split ONCE, for the final triad report only. (2) All selection/tuning on VAL (here acdc, itself a domain-shift split — good). (3) Prefer PARAMETERLESS / physics-derived choices over dataset-fitted ones: deriving a knob from dataset statistics (blood_scale=1.6 'fidelity-found', jitter=0.4) is ALSO a soft leak — the generator absorbs our-data stats. Physical mechanisms with CITED literature params (inflow f=v*TR/thk; T1/T2 from Stanisz/Bojorquez) are leak-free because the numbers come from physics/literature, not our data. A decision made on physical grounds (cine blood IS inflow-enhanced) needs no test number at all. Applies to all projects.

SYNTH REPORTING TAXONOMY (owner 2026-07-02, MUST state scope upfront): synth results decompose on TWO axes - SHAPE (anatomy) and COLOR (contrast/appearance) - x CONTROLLED vs UNCONTROLLED. (1) REPAINT = real masks (SHAPE given) + randomized paint -> measures COLOR generalization ALONE (shape held real), ~0.68 cross-vendor. (2) GENERATE = synth Rodero masks (SHAPE generated) + randomized paint -> full SHAPE+COLOR generation, ~0.56. (3) INVERSE/ncph = real shape + CONTROLLED color (fit to a scan) -> color FIDELITY. Key: 0.68(repaint) - 0.56(generate) ~= 0.12 = the isolated COST OF GENERATING SHAPE (color constant). Neither is cheating - repaint is the color-axis-only metric, generate is both. RULE: always say upfront which shape source (real=repaint vs synth=generate) and which color mode (uncontrolled/randomized vs controlled/fit). Per-class intensity W1 = color-axis metric only (anatomy-agnostic); shape/generation quality needs spatial embedding (uy4d). Dont present repaint numbers as 'generation'.

Cross-platform data root: core.paths.resolve_data_root adapts the ONE configured root (paths.yaml/CARDIAC_DATA) to running OS. Native-first: WSL 'wslpath' tool for Win<->mount translation, pure PureWindows/PurePosixPath fallback. RAISES on Windows-path-on-native-linux (never goes relative). WHY: a Windows path on POSIX is treated relative -> silently creates repo-local 'D:/' dir (NTFS uses U+F03A for ':'), which leaked dataset metadata into the public git history once. config._root delegates to it. Tests inject os_name/wsl (DI, not monkeypatch). Also scrub machine-specific absolute paths from tracked files - they expose fs floor-map, no portfolio value.
